text: The implementation is broader than a renderer-only icon swap: it also replaces tracked-change parsing and regenerates a large portion of the lockfile. I’m checking those two areas against actual Git output and npm metadata now, because they carry the main regression risk.

text:
{
  "name": "pi-desktop",
  "version": "0.6.5",
  "description": "PiDeck is an open-source desktop workbench to manage multiple pi AI coding agents across local project folders with session history, Git integration, terminal, and config management.",
  "private": true,
  "license": "MIT",
  "author": {
    "name": "ayuayue",
    "email": "ayuayue@users.noreply.github.com"
  },
  "contributors": [
    {
      "name": "1900EasonJin",
      "url": "https://github.com/1900EasonJin"
    }
  ],
  "homepage": "https://github.com/ayuayue/PiDeck#readme",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/ayuayue/PiDeck.git"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/ayuayue/PiDeck/issues"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "pi",
    "pi-agent",
    "ai-coding-agent",
    "coding-agent",
    "electron",
    "electron-app",
    "desktop",
    "desktop-app",
    "rpc",
    "agent",
    "ai-assistant",
    "local-ai",
    "developer-tools",
    "productivity",
    "workspace",
    "session-management",
    "git",
    "terminal",
    "monaco-editor",
    "claude-code",
    "codex",
    "multi-project",
    "typescript",
    "windows",
    "macos",
    "linux",
    "open-source"
  ],
  "main": "out/main/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "node scripts/dev.js",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "pack": "npm run build && electron-builder --dir",
    "dist": "npm run build && electron-builder",
    "dist:win": "npm run build && electron-builder --win nsis portable zip",
    "dist:mac": "npm run build && electron-builder --mac dmg zip",
    "dist:linux": "npm run build && electron-builder --linux AppImage deb tar.gz",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "docs:dev": "vitepress dev docs-site",
    "docs:build": "vitepress build docs-site",
    "docs:preview": "vitepress preview docs-site",
    "postinstall": "node scripts/fix-pty-permissions.js",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "make-icon": "node scripts/make-icon.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@electron-toolkit/utils": "^4.0.0",
    "@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk": "^1.67.0",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.1.19",
    "node-pty": "^1.1.0",
    "seti-icons": "^0.0.4",
    "sonner": "^2.0.7"
  },
  "build": {
    "appId": "com.ayuayue.pi-desktop",
    "productName": "PiDeck",
    "asar": true,
    "compression": "maximum",
    "afterPack": "./scripts/after-pack-cleanup.js",
    "npmRebuild": false,
    "directories": {
      "output": "release",
      "buildResources": "build"
    },
    "icon": "build/icon",
    "files": [
      "out/**/*",
      "package.json"
    ],
    "extraResources": [
      {
        "from": "resources/extensions",
        "to": "extensions",
        "filter": [
          "*.ts"
        ]
      },
      {
        "from": "resources/yao-prompts",
        "to": "yao-prompts"
      },
      {
        "from": "build/pets",
        "to": "pets",
        "filter": [
          "**/*"
        ]
      }
    ],
    "extraMetadata": {
      "main": "out/main/index.js"
    },
    "win": {
      "icon": "build/icon.ico",
      "target": [
        "nsis",
        "portable",
        "zip"
      ]
    },
    "nsis": {
      "oneClick": false,
      "allowToChangeInstallationDirectory": true,
      "createDesktopShortcut": true,
      "createStartMenuShortcut": true
    },
    "mac": {
      "icon": "build/icon.icns",
      "target": [
        "dmg",
        "zip"
      ],
      "category": "public.app-category.developer-tools"
    },
    "linux": {
      "icon": "build/icons",
      "target": [
        "AppImage",
        "deb",
        "tar.gz"
      ],
      "category": "Development",
      "maintainer": "ayuayue <ayuayue@users.noreply.github.com>"
    }
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@electron-toolkit/preload": "^3.0.2",
    "@fiahfy/icns": "^0.0.7",
    "@monaco-editor/react": "^4.7.0",
    "@types/node": "^24.0.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^5.0.0",
    "@xterm/addon-fit": "^0.11.0",
    "@xterm/xterm": "^6.0.0",
    "electron": "^38.8.6",
    "electron-builder": "^26.8.1",
    "electron-vite": "^4.0.0",
    "eslint": "^10.6.0",
    "html-to-image": "^1.11.13",
    "katex": "^0.17.0",
    "lucide-react": "^1.17.0",
    "mermaid": "^11.15.0",
    "monaco-editor": "^0.55.1",
    "png-to-ico": "^3.0.1",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "rehype-katex": "^7.0.1",
    "remark-breaks": "^4.0.0",
    "remark-gfm": "^4.0.1",
    "remark-highlight-mark": "^1.4.0",
    "remark-math": "^6.0.0",
    "sharp": "^0.34.5",
    "typescript": "^5.9.0",
    "unist-util-visit": "^5.1.0",
    "vite": "^7.3.5",
    "vitepress": "^1.6.4",
    "zustand": "^5.0.0"
  }
}

content.text: The focused tests and TypeScript check pass, but two substantive issues are now confirmed: extensionless names are mislabeled due to `slice(-1)`, and the new direct dependency brings an unfixable high-severity `svgo` chain into production. I’m doing one clean build retry and checking whether the test is actually wired into project validation before finalizing severity.

arguments.command: node -e "const f=n=>{const base=n.split('/').pop()?.split('\\\\').pop()??n;const ext=n.slice(n.lastIndexOf('.')).toLowerCase();return ext?(ext.slice(1).toUpperCase()):'File'}; for(const n of ['Makefile','src','CMakeLists','README']) console.log(n,'=>',f(n))"

git show HEAD:package-lock.json | grep -c 'npmmirror.com'; grep -c 'npmmirror.com' package-lock.json; git show HEAD:package-lock.json | grep -c 'registry.npmjs.org'; grep -c 'registry.npmjs.org' package-lock.json
git diff -- package-lock.json | grep -E '^[+-].*resolved.*(npmmirror|npmjs)' | awk '{print substr($0,1,1), ($0 ~ /npmmirror/ ? "mirror" : "npmjs")}' | sort | uniq -c
git diff -- package-lock.json | grep '^[-+]      "peer"' | sort | uniq -c; git diff -- package-lock.json | grep '^[-+]      "dev"' | sort | uniq -c
grep -n -- '--color-success' src/renderer/src/styles.css | head -n 10; nl -ba package.json | sed -n '68,80p'; nl -ba package-lock.json | sed -n '12130,12160p'
git status --porcelain=v1 | awk 'substr($0,1,2)!="??" {print substr($0,1,2), substr($0,4)}'; git diff --cached --name-only

content.text: Review is complete. The feature-specific suite passes and the code typechecks, but the PR is not ready upstream because the chosen package introduces a new high-severity production dependency chain with no npm-provided fix; the review will also document nested-repository path handling, label/i18n/accessibility defects, and the non-minimal lockfile churn.

arguments.content:
## Review

Blocking findings are present. The current diff should not be sent upstream until the dependency finding is resolved.

### Blocker

- **The new direct dependency adds a known high-severity production vulnerability with no available fix.** `package.json:76` adds `seti-icons@^0.0.4`; `package-lock.json:12138-12146` shows that it unconditionally depends on the obsolete `svgo@^1.3.2`, and `package-lock.json:12649-12668` locks deprecated `svgo@1.3.2`. `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json` reports `seti-icons`, `svgo`, `css-select`, and `nth-check` as a high-severity chain with `fixAvailable: false`; `nth-check@1.0.2` is visible at `package-lock.json:10770-10777`. These packages were absent from `HEAD`. The package was last published in 2020 and declares build-time SVG tooling as runtime dependencies even though this renderer only consumes generated JSON/SVG. **Smallest fix:** do not ship `seti-icons` as a production dependency. Use a maintained runtime-only Seti data/icon package, or vendor the generated definitions/icons actually needed with the upstream MIT license/attribution and no SVGO runtime tree. Then regenerate the lockfile and rerun audit.

### High

- **Git badge paths are wrong when a project directory is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` executes Git from the supplied project `cwd`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves every returned path against that `cwd`. Verified in this repository: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` still returned root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the current code would turn those into `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. Those paths cannot match file-tree node paths, so `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently produces ineffective badges (and the changed-files list points at wrong files) for nested projects. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run the three listing commands from that root, join returned paths to that root, and, if the drawer is intended to be project-scoped, filter results to descendants of the selected project. Add a test where `cwd` is a nested directory.

### Medium

- **Extensionless files receive an empty type label, and directories get a misleading file-type tooltip.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:37-46` calls `slice(fileName.lastIndexOf("."))`; when no dot exists, `lastIndexOf` is `-1`, so `Makefile` produces `"e"`, then `ext.slice(1)` produces `""` instead of `"File"` or `"Makefile"`. `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4296` computes this label for directories too, and `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4321-4324` appends it to every directory title. Plain directories therefore gain a blank extra line, while dotted directories can be announced as arbitrary file types. **Smallest fix:** derive the dot index from `base`, explicitly handle `dotIndex <= 0`, add special names such as `Makefile` if desired, and only compute/append file type text for `node.type === "file"`.

- **The new status/type UI is not complete for localization, keyboard use, or non-color perception.** User-visible labels are hardcoded in `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:32-46` (notably `File`, `Video`, `Audio`, `Archive`, `Font`, and `License`) rather than going through `i18n.ts`. The badge at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4310-4313` is an empty span whose status is conveyed only through the colors at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:87-90`; neither the button title nor an accessible label exposes added/modified/renamed state. The hover label selector at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:103-105` also omits `:focus-visible`, so keyboard focus does not reveal the same information. **Smallest fix:** keep technical identifiers such as TypeScript/JSON as data, move translatable category and Git-status text to i18n keys, expose the status in the button's accessible description or visually-hidden text, mark decorative SVG/badge content `aria-hidden`, and mirror hover with `.file-node-row:focus-visible .file-node-type-label` (plus reduced-motion handling if retaining the transition).

- **The Git path change is only source-pattern tested, leaving the parser's claimed edge cases unverified.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:37-42` reads `GitService.ts` as text and checks three regexes; it never executes the parser with NUL-delimited modified/renamed/copied records. It therefore passes despite the nested-root defect above. The untracked command at `src/main/git/GitService.ts:121-124` also still lacks `-z`, and `src/main/git/GitService.ts:160-162` splits on newlines and trims, corrupting valid untracked names containing newlines or leading/trailing spaces. **Smallest fix:** extract/export a pure `parseNameStatusZ` helper, use `git ls-files -z` for untracked files, and test actual buffers/strings for spaces, Unicode, tabs/newlines, rename scores, copies, and nested `cwd` behavior.

- **The lockfile includes substantial unrelated resolver/registry churn.** The package-lock diff is `+1751/-95` lines for one direct dependency, including 37 peer-flag changes, 22 dev-flag changes, extra optional React 18 nodes, and 124 changed/added `resolved` lines (predominantly `npmmirror.com`). This obscures the dependency review and widens an otherwise focused feature. The newly required old SVGO tree explains some additions, but not the peer/dev metadata and registry rewrites across unrelated packages. **Smallest fix:** after replacing the vulnerable package, regenerate from clean `HEAD` using the repository's agreed npm version/registry and retain only dependency-closure changes. Consider pinning the npm version via `packageManager` for reproducible locks.

### Low

- **Seti lookup is case-sensitive, so uppercase extensions fall back to the generic icon.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:21-23` passes the original filename directly to `getIcon`. Runtime verification showed `getIcon("App.TSX")` and `getIcon("README.MD")` return the generic white icon, while lowercase equivalents return the blue TSX/Markdown icons. **Smallest fix:** normalize the lookup name to lowercase (the package recognizes lowercase `dockerfile` and `makefile`) and add uppercase-extension coverage.

### Correct

- `src/renderer/src/main.tsx:5-6` imports the icon stylesheet after the base stylesheet, so the narrowly scoped file-tree overrides apply predictably.
- `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4267-4280` uses static package-provided SVG rather than filename/user-provided markup and retains a `FileText` fallback. The Seti wrapper maps the package's declared color names to CSS variables at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:7-27`.
- File-tree indentation remains depth-based and recursive status maps are forwarded at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4333-4341`; TypeScript accepts the React/CSS custom-property usage.
- Existing row interaction states remain in `src/renderer/src/styles.css:6362-6371` (`hover`, `active`, and `focus-visible`), and new icon colors include a dark-theme palette at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:29-46`.
- No staged files were present at the end of review (`git diff --cached --name-only` was empty). Project/source files were not modified by this review.

### Validation Notes

- `node --test tests/fileIcons.test.mjs`: passed, 5/5.
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `npm ls seti-icons --all`: passed; resolved exactly `seti-icons@0.0.4`.
- `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json`: failed with 8 total high-severity findings; importantly, four newly introduced nodes are the direct `seti-icons -> svgo -> css-select -> nth-check` chain with no available fix. Other audit findings are pre-existing packages and are outside this feature review.
- `node --test tests/*.test.mjs`: feature tests passed, but the aggregate run failed with 89/92 passing. Failures were `agent startup writes diagnostics...`, `builds multiple fetched models...`, and `RichInput inserts multiline text...`; none points at a file changed by this feature, but the branch is not globally green.
- `npm run build` first terminated with exit 139 during client transformation. A direct `npx electron-vite build` retry progressed further but failed parsing `node_modules/monaco-editor/.../typescriptServices.js`, outside the changed files. Thus a full production build is not validated in this workspace.
- No visual/manual Electron check was performed; narrow drawer widths, dark mode, keyboard focus, and screen-reader output remain residual UI risks.

```acceptance-report

    },
    {

text:
## Review

Blocking findings are present. The current diff should not be sent upstream until the dependency finding is resolved.

### Blocker

- **The new direct dependency adds a known high-severity production vulnerability with no available fix.** `package.json:76` adds `seti-icons@^0.0.4`; `package-lock.json:12138-12146` shows that it unconditionally depends on the obsolete `svgo@^1.3.2`, and `package-lock.json:12649-12668` locks deprecated `svgo@1.3.2`. `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json` reports `seti-icons`, `svgo`, `css-select`, and `nth-check` as a high-severity chain with `fixAvailable: false`; `nth-check@1.0.2` is visible at `package-lock.json:10770-10777`. These packages were absent from `HEAD`. The package was last published in 2020 and declares build-time SVG tooling as runtime dependencies even though this renderer only consumes generated JSON/SVG. **Smallest fix:** do not ship `seti-icons` as a production dependency. Use a maintained runtime-only Seti data/icon package, or vendor the generated definitions/icons actually needed with the upstream MIT license/attribution and no SVGO runtime tree. Then regenerate the lockfile and rerun audit.

### High

- **Git badge paths are wrong when a project directory is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` executes Git from the supplied project `cwd`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves every returned path against that `cwd`. Verified in this repository: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` still returned root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the current code would turn those into `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. Those paths cannot match file-tree node paths, so `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently produces ineffective badges (and the changed-files list points at wrong files) for nested projects. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run the three listing commands from that root, join returned paths to that root, and, if the drawer is intended to be project-scoped, filter results to descendants of the selected project. Add a test where `cwd` is a nested directory.

### Medium

- **Extensionless files receive an empty type label, and directories get a misleading file-type tooltip.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:37-46` calls `slice(fileName.lastIndexOf("."))`; when no dot exists, `lastIndexOf` is `-1`, so `Makefile` produces `"e"`, then `ext.slice(1)` produces `""` instead of `"File"` or `"Makefile"`. `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4296` computes this label for directories too, and `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4321-4324` appends it to every directory title. Plain directories therefore gain a blank extra line, while dotted directories can be announced as arbitrary file types. **Smallest fix:** derive the dot index from `base`, explicitly handle `dotIndex <= 0`, add special names such as `Makefile` if desired, and only compute/append file type text for `node.type === "file"`.

- **The new status/type UI is not complete for localization, keyboard use, or non-color perception.** User-visible labels are hardcoded in `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:32-46` (notably `File`, `Video`, `Audio`, `Archive`, `Font`, and `License`) rather than going through `i18n.ts`. The badge at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4310-4313` is an empty span whose status is conveyed only through the colors at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:87-90`; neither the button title nor an accessible label exposes added/modified/renamed state. The hover label selector at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:103-105` also omits `:focus-visible`, so keyboard focus does not reveal the same information. **Smallest fix:** keep technical identifiers such as TypeScript/JSON as data, move translatable category and Git-status text to i18n keys, expose the status in the button's accessible description or visually-hidden text, mark decorative SVG/badge content `aria-hidden`, and mirror hover with `.file-node-row:focus-visible .file-node-type-label` (plus reduced-motion handling if retaining the transition).

- **The Git path change is only source-pattern tested, leaving the parser's claimed edge cases unverified.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:37-42` reads `GitService.ts` as text and checks three regexes; it never executes the parser with NUL-delimited modified/renamed/copied records. It therefore passes despite the nested-root defect above. The untracked command at `src/main/git/GitService.ts:121-124` also still lacks `-z`, and `src/main/git/GitService.ts:160-162` splits on newlines and trims, corrupting valid untracked names containing newlines or leading/trailing spaces. **Smallest fix:** extract/export a pure `parseNameStatusZ` helper, use `git ls-files -z` for untracked files, and test actual buffers/strings for spaces, Unicode, tabs/newlines, rename scores, copies, and nested `cwd` behavior.

- **The lockfile includes substantial unrelated resolver/registry churn.** The package-lock diff is `+1751/-95` lines for one direct dependency, including 37 peer-flag changes, 22 dev-flag changes, extra optional React 18 nodes, and 124 changed/added `resolved` lines (predominantly `npmmirror.com`). This obscures the dependency review and widens an otherwise focused feature. The newly required old SVGO tree explains some additions, but not the peer/dev metadata and registry rewrites across unrelated packages. **Smallest fix:** after replacing the vulnerable package, regenerate from clean `HEAD` using the repository's agreed npm version/registry and retain only dependency-closure changes. Consider pinning the npm version via `packageManager` for reproducible locks.

### Low

- **Seti lookup is case-sensitive, so uppercase extensions fall back to the generic icon.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:21-23` passes the original filename directly to `getIcon`. Runtime verification showed `getIcon("App.TSX")` and `getIcon("README.MD")` return the generic white icon, while lowercase equivalents return the blue TSX/Markdown icons. **Smallest fix:** normalize the lookup name to lowercase (the package recognizes lowercase `dockerfile` and `makefile`) and add uppercase-extension coverage.

### Correct

- `src/renderer/src/main.tsx:5-6` imports the icon stylesheet after the base stylesheet, so the narrowly scoped file-tree overrides apply predictably.
- `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4267-4280` uses static package-provided SVG rather than filename/user-provided markup and retains a `FileText` fallback. The Seti wrapper maps the package's declared color names to CSS variables at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:7-27`.
- File-tree indentation remains depth-based and recursive status maps are forwarded at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4333-4341`; TypeScript accepts the React/CSS custom-property usage.
- Existing row interaction states remain in `src/renderer/src/styles.css:6362-6371` (`hover`, `active`, and `focus-visible`), and new icon colors include a dark-theme palette at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:29-46`.
- No staged files were present at the end of review (`git diff --cached --name-only` was empty). Project/source files were not modified by this review.

### Validation Notes

- `node --test tests/fileIcons.test.mjs`: passed, 5/5.
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `npm ls seti-icons --all`: passed; resolved exactly `seti-icons@0.0.4`.
- `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json`: failed with 8 total high-severity findings; importantly, four newly introduced nodes are the direct `seti-icons -> svgo -> css-select -> nth-check` chain with no available fix. Other audit findings are pre-existing packages and are outside this feature review.
- `node --test tests/*.test.mjs`: feature tests passed, but the aggregate run failed with 89/92 passing. Failures were `agent startup writes diagnostics...`, `builds multiple fetched models...`, and `RichInput inserts multiline text...`; none points at a file changed by this feature, but the branch is not globally green.
- `npm run build` first terminated with exit 139 during client transformation. A direct `npx electron-vite build` retry progressed further but failed parsing `node_modules/monaco-editor/.../typescriptServices.js`, outside the changed files. Thus a full production build is not validated in this workspace.
- No visual/manual Electron check was performed; narrow drawer widths, dark mode, keyboard focus, and screen-reader output remain residual UI risks.

```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "not-satisfied",
      "evidence": "The Seti feature works at typecheck/focused-test level, but scope is widened by Git parser changes and +1751/-95 lockfile churn, and the chosen direct dependency adds an unfixable high-severity production vulnerability."
    },
    {
      "id": "criterion-2",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "Reviewed all eight feature files with exact line evidence; ran focused and aggregate tests, typecheck, diff checks, dependency resolution, production audit, build attempts, Git-path probes, and staged-state checks."
    }

text:
# Seti icon data

The SVG and mapping data in this directory is a snapshot of the Seti-UI file icons used by VS Code.

- Upstream icon project: https://github.com/jesseweed/seti-ui
- Data extraction source: `seti-icons` 0.0.4 by Elvis Wolcott (https://github.com/elviswolcott/seti-icons)
- Snapshot files: `definitions.json` and `icons.json`
- License: MIT; see `LICENSE.md`

The lookup implementation in `index.ts` is maintained locally so the desktop renderer does not install the obsolete build-time dependency tree published as runtime dependencies by `seti-icons` 0.0.4.

content.text:
# Seti icon data

The SVG and mapping data in this directory is a snapshot of the Seti-UI file icons used by VS Code.

- Upstream icon project: https://github.com/jesseweed/seti-ui
- Data extraction source: `seti-icons` 0.0.4 by Elvis Wolcott (https://github.com/elviswolcott/seti-icons)
- Snapshot files: `definitions.json` and `icons.json`
- License: MIT; see `LICENSE.md`

The lookup implementation in `index.ts` is maintained locally so the desktop renderer does not install the obsolete build-time dependency tree published as runtime dependencies by `seti-icons` 0.0.4.

arguments.message: UPDATE: During final artifact verification, a concurrent untracked src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/ directory appeared (timestamps ~23:18-23:19). It contains vendored definitions/icons, local lookup, MIT license/notice, apparently intended to replace the vulnerable seti-icons dependency. It is not yet wired in: package.json/package-lock and fileIcons.ts still use seti-icons. My current review artifact reflects the still-active dependency and notes the blocker; changedFiles list does not yet include the newly appearing vendor files.

arguments.content:
## Review

Blocking findings are present. The current diff is not ready for an upstream PR.

### Blocker

- **The new vendored Seti module does not typecheck, so the required build cannot pass.** `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:25` asserts imported JSON directly to `SetiDefinitions`, but TypeScript infers each JSON mapping value as `string[]`, which is not safely comparable to the fixed two-element `IconDetails` tuple. `npm run typecheck` fails with TS2352 at this line. The focused test still passes because `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:5-16` parses the JSON independently and never imports or compiles `index.ts`. **Smallest fix:** either validate/narrow the JSON structure, provide JSON declarations that preserve tuple types, or, if the bundled immutable data is accepted as trusted, make the explicit boundary `rawDefinitions as unknown as SetiDefinitions`; then rerun typecheck and the full build.

### High

- **Git badge paths are wrong when a project directory is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` executes Git from the supplied project `cwd`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves every returned path against that `cwd`. Verified in this repository: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` still returned repository-root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the current code would create `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. Those paths cannot match file-tree nodes, so the map at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently produces no badges for nested projects and changed-file actions point to wrong paths. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run the listing commands from that root, join returned paths to that root, and filter to the selected project subtree if the drawer is intended to remain project-scoped. Add a nested-`cwd` integration test.

### Medium

- **Directory tooltips are now mislabeled as files.** `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4296` computes `getFileTypeLabel(node.name)` for both files and directories; `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4321-4324` appends that value to directory titles. Because extensionless names return `FILE` at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:39-40`, a normal `src` directory is exposed as `src\nFILE`; dotted directories can receive arbitrary extension labels. **Smallest fix:** compute and append the type label only in the file branch. Keep the directory title as its relative path (or add a localized directory description if product requirements call for one).

- **The new status/type UI is incomplete for localization, keyboard use, and non-color perception.** User-visible category text is hardcoded at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:33-50`, including `FILE`, rather than going through `i18n.ts`. The badge at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4310-4313` is an empty span whose added/modified/renamed state is conveyed only by colors at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:87-90`; neither the button title nor an accessible description exposes the status. The label CSS at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:103-105` reacts to hover but not keyboard focus. **Smallest fix:** keep true technical identifiers (TypeScript, JSON, TSX) as data, localize generic categories and Git status names, add status text through an accessible description or visually hidden span, mark decorative SVG/badge content `aria-hidden`, mirror hover with `.file-node-row:focus-visible .file-node-type-label`, and respect reduced motion for the opacity transition.

- **The Git parser tests inspect source text rather than behavior and omit edge cases the code claims to handle.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:56-61` only regex-matches implementation source, so it passes despite the nested-root defect. The untracked command at `src/main/git/GitService.ts:121-124` also lacks `-z`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:160-162` splits on newlines and trims; valid untracked names containing newlines or leading/trailing spaces are corrupted. **Smallest fix:** extract/export a pure NUL-status parser, use `git ls-files -z` for untracked files, and execute tests for spaces, Unicode, tabs/newlines, rename scores, copies, staged/unstaged duplicates, and nested working directories.

- **The focused Seti lookup test duplicates a simplified implementation instead of testing the production lookup.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:12-16` checks only an exact filename or one final suffix and does not execute `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:30-44`, including multi-part extensions and partial matches. This is why the focused suite is green while production TypeScript is broken. **Smallest fix:** make the lookup test import compiled production code through the repository's established test loader/build path, or factor the runtime lookup into a directly executable module. Cover multi-part extensions, partials, default fallback, and malformed/missing icon data.

### Low

- **Seti lookup remains case-sensitive, so uppercase extensions fall back to the generic icon.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:21-23` forwards the original name, and `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:30-42` performs exact case-sensitive lookups. Runtime checks against the source data showed uppercase `App.TSX`/`README.MD` miss mappings that lowercase equivalents resolve. **Smallest fix:** normalize the lookup name to lowercase (the data recognizes lowercase `dockerfile` and `makefile`) and add uppercase-extension tests.

- **The new type-label column permanently reserves invisible width.** `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:49-50` adds an `auto` third grid column, while `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:93-104` hides it only with `opacity`. This correctly avoids hover layout shift, but on a narrow right drawer a long label such as `TypeScript` still truncates the filename even when the label is invisible. **Smallest fix:** cap the label width and hide it below a drawer/container-width threshold, preserving the stable grid and no-text-jump rule.

### Correct

- The dependency/lockfile issue discovered in the initial snapshot has been removed: final `package.json` and `package-lock.json` match `HEAD`, and the implementation now uses a local Seti snapshot. `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/NOTICE.md:1-8` identifies both upstream sources and `LICENSE.md:1-21` includes the MIT license. The final audit contains no `seti-icons`/SVGO findings; its four high findings are pre-existing dependencies outside this diff.
- `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:30-50` now handles extensionless names without the prior `slice(-1)` empty-label bug and normalizes special-name label matching.
- `src/renderer/src/main.tsx:5-6` imports the dedicated stylesheet after base styles, so its scoped overrides apply predictably.
- `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4267-4280` renders only trusted bundled SVG and retains a `FileText` fallback. The color mapping is exhaustively typed at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:7-27`.
- Recursive Git status maps are forwarded at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4333-4341`, and existing row hover/active/focus-visible styling remains at `src/renderer/src/styles.css:6362-6371`.
- `git diff --check` passes. No staged files were present at the end of review. Project/source files were not modified by this reviewer; only this required artifact was written.

### Validation Notes

- `node --test tests/fileIcons.test.mjs`: passed, 6/6, but it does not compile/import the production TypeScript lookup.
- `npm run typecheck`: **failed** with TS2352 at `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:25`.
- A full current-snapshot build was not rerun because its first `tsc --noEmit` step is already known to fail. Earlier build attempts, before the concurrent vendoring correction, also did not complete due workspace/Monaco issues and are not evidence for the final snapshot.
- `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json`: reports four pre-existing high-severity dependency findings and no Seti/SVGO chain.
- `node --test tests/*.test.mjs` was run before the final concurrent vendoring correction: the Seti tests passed, while three unrelated existing tests failed. It is not a final-snapshot green run.
- No manual Electron UI check was performed; narrow widths, dark mode, keyboard focus, and screen-reader output remain residual risks.

details._piDeckOriginalContent:
## Review

Blocking findings are present. The current diff should not be sent upstream until the dependency finding is resolved.

### Blocker

- **The new direct dependency adds a known high-severity production vulnerability with no available fix.** `package.json:76` adds `seti-icons@^0.0.4`; `package-lock.json:12138-12146` shows that it unconditionally depends on the obsolete `svgo@^1.3.2`, and `package-lock.json:12649-12668` locks deprecated `svgo@1.3.2`. `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json` reports `seti-icons`, `svgo`, `css-select`, and `nth-check` as a high-severity chain with `fixAvailable: false`; `nth-check@1.0.2` is visible at `package-lock.json:10770-10777`. These packages were absent from `HEAD`. The package was last published in 2020 and declares build-time SVG tooling as runtime dependencies even though this renderer only consumes generated JSON/SVG. **Smallest fix:** do not ship `seti-icons` as a production dependency. Use a maintained runtime-only Seti data/icon package, or vendor the generated definitions/icons actually needed with the upstream MIT license/attribution and no SVGO runtime tree. Then regenerate the lockfile and rerun audit.

### High

- **Git badge paths are wrong when a project directory is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` executes Git from the supplied project `cwd`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves every returned path against that `cwd`. Verified in this repository: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` still returned root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the current code would turn those into `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. Those paths cannot match file-tree node paths, so `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently produces ineffective badges (and the changed-files list points at wrong files) for nested projects. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run the three listing commands from that root, join returned paths to that root, and, if the drawer is intended to be project-scoped, filter results to descendants of the selected project. Add a test where `cwd` is a nested directory.

### Medium

- **Extensionless files receive an empty type label, and directories get a misleading file-type tooltip.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:37-46` calls `slice(fileName.lastIndexOf("."))`; when no dot exists, `lastIndexOf` is `-1`, so `Makefile` produces `"e"`, then `ext.slice(1)` produces `""` instead of `"File"` or `"Makefile"`. `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4296` computes this label for directories too, and `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4321-4324` appends it to every directory title. Plain directories therefore gain a blank extra line, while dotted directories can be announced as arbitrary file types. **Smallest fix:** derive the dot index from `base`, explicitly handle `dotIndex <= 0`, add special names such as `Makefile` if desired, and only compute/append file type text for `node.type === "file"`.

- **The new status/type UI is not complete for localization, keyboard use, or non-color perception.** User-visible labels are hardcoded in `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:32-46` (notably `File`, `Video`, `Audio`, `Archive`, `Font`, and `License`) rather than going through `i18n.ts`. The badge at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4310-4313` is an empty span whose status is conveyed only through the colors at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:87-90`; neither the button title nor an accessible label exposes added/modified/renamed state. The hover label selector at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:103-105` also omits `:focus-visible`, so keyboard focus does not reveal the same information. **Smallest fix:** keep technical identifiers such as TypeScript/JSON as data, move translatable category and Git-status text to i18n keys, expose the status in the button's accessible description or visually-hidden text, mark decorative SVG/badge content `aria-hidden`, and mirror hover with `.file-node-row:focus-visible .file-node-type-label` (plus reduced-motion handling if retaining the transition).

- **The Git path change is only source-pattern tested, leaving the parser's claimed edge cases unverified.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:37-42` reads `GitService.ts` as text and checks three regexes; it never executes the parser with NUL-delimited modified/renamed/copied records. It therefore passes despite the nested-root defect above. The untracked command at `src/main/git/GitService.ts:121-124` also still lacks `-z`, and `src/main/git/GitService.ts:160-162` splits on newlines and trims, corrupting valid untracked names containing newlines or leading/trailing spaces. **Smallest fix:** extract/export a pure `parseNameStatusZ` helper, use `git ls-files -z` for untracked files, and test actual buffers/strings for spaces, Unicode, tabs/newlines, rename scores, copies, and nested `cwd` behavior.

- **The lockfile includes substantial unrelated resolver/registry churn.** The package-lock diff is `+1751/-95` lines for one direct dependency, including 37 peer-flag changes, 22 dev-flag changes, extra optional React 18 nodes, and 124 changed/added `resolved` lines (predominantly `npmmirror.com`). This obscures the dependency review and widens an otherwise focused feature. The newly required old SVGO tree explains some additions, but not the peer/dev metadata and registry rewrites across unrelated packages. **Smallest fix:** after replacing the vulnerable package, regenerate from clean `HEAD` using the repository's agreed npm version/registry and retain only dependency-closure changes. Consider pinning the npm version via `packageManager` for reproducible locks.

### Low

- **Seti lookup is case-sensitive, so uppercase extensions fall back to the generic icon.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:21-23` passes the original filename directly to `getIcon`. Runtime verification showed `getIcon("App.TSX")` and `getIcon("README.MD")` return the generic white icon, while lowercase equivalents return the blue TSX/Markdown icons. **Smallest fix:** normalize the lookup name to lowercase (the package recognizes lowercase `dockerfile` and `makefile`) and add uppercase-extension coverage.

### Correct

- `src/renderer/src/main.tsx:5-6` imports the icon stylesheet after the base stylesheet, so the narrowly scoped file-tree overrides apply predictably.
- `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4267-4280` uses static package-provided SVG rather than filename/user-provided markup and retains a `FileText` fallback. The Seti wrapper maps the package's declared color names to CSS variables at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:7-27`.
- File-tree indentation remains depth-based and recursive status maps are forwarded at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4333-4341`; TypeScript accepts the React/CSS custom-property usage.
- Existing row interaction states remain in `src/renderer/src/styles.css:6362-6371` (`hover`, `active`, and `focus-visible`), and new icon colors include a dark-theme palette at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:29-46`.
- No staged files were present at the end of review (`git diff --cached --name-only` was empty). Project/source files were not modified by this review.

### Validation Notes

- `node --test tests/fileIcons.test.mjs`: passed, 5/5.
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `npm ls seti-icons --all`: passed; resolved exactly `seti-icons@0.0.4`.
- `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json`: failed with 8 total high-severity findings; importantly, four newly introduced nodes are the direct `seti-icons -> svgo -> css-select -> nth-check` chain with no available fix. Other audit findings are pre-existing packages and are outside this feature review.
- `node --test tests/*.test.mjs`: feature tests passed, but the aggregate run failed with 89/92 passing. Failures were `agent startup writes diagnostics...`, `builds multiple fetched models...`, and `RichInput inserts multiline text...`; none points at a file changed by this feature, but the branch is not globally green.
- `npm run build` first terminated with exit 139 during client transformation. A direct `npx electron-vite build` retry progressed further but failed parsing `node_modules/monaco-editor/.../typescriptServices.js`, outside the changed files. Thus a full production build is not validated in this workspace.
- No visual/manual Electron check was performed; narrow drawer widths, dark mode, keyboard focus, and screen-reader output remain residual UI risks.

text:
## Review

Blocking findings are present. The current diff is not ready for an upstream PR.

### Blocker

- **The new vendored Seti module does not typecheck, so the required build cannot pass.** `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:25` asserts imported JSON directly to `SetiDefinitions`, but TypeScript infers each JSON mapping value as `string[]`, which is not safely comparable to the fixed two-element `IconDetails` tuple. `npm run typecheck` fails with TS2352 at this line. The focused test still passes because `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:5-16` parses the JSON independently and never imports or compiles `index.ts`. **Smallest fix:** either validate/narrow the JSON structure, provide JSON declarations that preserve tuple types, or, if the bundled immutable data is accepted as trusted, make the explicit boundary `rawDefinitions as unknown as SetiDefinitions`; then rerun typecheck and the full build.

### High

- **Git badge paths are wrong when a project directory is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` executes Git from the supplied project `cwd`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves every returned path against that `cwd`. Verified in this repository: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` still returned repository-root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the current code would create `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. Those paths cannot match file-tree nodes, so the map at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently produces no badges for nested projects and changed-file actions point to wrong paths. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run the listing commands from that root, join returned paths to that root, and filter to the selected project subtree if the drawer is intended to remain project-scoped. Add a nested-`cwd` integration test.

## Review

Blocking findings are present. The current diff is not ready for an upstream PR.

### Blocker

- **The new vendored Seti module does not typecheck, so the required build cannot pass.** `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:25` asserts imported JSON directly to `SetiDefinitions`, but TypeScript infers each JSON mapping value as `string[]`, which is not safely comparable to the fixed two-element `IconDetails` tuple. `npm run typecheck` fails with TS2352 at this line. The focused test still passes because `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:5-16` parses the JSON independently and never imports or compiles `index.ts`. **Smallest fix:** either validate/narrow the JSON structure, provide JSON declarations that preserve tuple types, or, if the bundled immutable data is accepted as trusted, make the explicit boundary `rawDefinitions as unknown as SetiDefinitions`; then rerun typecheck and the full build.

### High

- **Git badge paths are wrong when a project directory is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` executes Git from the supplied project `cwd`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves every returned path against that `cwd`. Verified in this repository: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` still returned repository-root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the current code would create `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. Those paths cannot match file-tree nodes, so the map at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently produces no badges for nested projects and changed-file actions point to wrong paths. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run the listing commands from that root, join returned paths to that root, and filter to the selected project subtree if the drawer is intended to remain project-scoped. Add a nested-`cwd` integration test.

## Review

**No blocking findings remain.** The dependency/lockfile issue and vendored-module TypeScript error seen during review were corrected in the latest state. The following important findings should still be addressed before or immediately after the upstream PR.

### High

- **Git badge paths are incorrect when the selected project is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` runs Git from the supplied project `cwd`, but `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves returned paths against that `cwd`. Verified here: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` returned repository-root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the implementation would produce `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. These cannot match file-tree node paths, so the status map at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently yields no badges for nested projects and changed-file actions target wrong paths. This underlying path assumption predates the new parser, but the new badge feature directly relies on it. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run listings from that root, join results to that root, and filter to the selected project subtree if the drawer is project-scoped. Add a nested-`cwd` integration test.

### Medium

- **Git status is color-only and inaccessible, and keyboard focus does not reveal the type label.** The badge at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4310-4313` is an empty span; added/modified/renamed state is conveyed only by colors at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:87-90`. Neither the button title nor an accessible description announces the status. The type label appears only on hover at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:103-105`, not on `:focus-visible`. **Smallest fix:** add localized Git status text via an accessible description or visually hidden span, mark decorative SVG/badge content `aria-hidden`, and mirror the hover selector for `.file-node-row:focus-visible`.

- **Directories are exposed as files in their tooltips, and the generic label bypasses i18n.** `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4296` computes a file type for every node; `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4321-4324` appends it to directory titles. A normal `src` directory therefore receives `src\nFILE` because `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:39-40` returns the hardcoded generic label `FILE`. **Smallest fix:** compute/append type labels only in the file branch. If a generic file category remains visible, route it through `i18n.ts`; technical identifiers such as TypeScript/JSON can remain data.

- **The tests do not execute the production Seti or Git parsing implementations.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:12-16` duplicates a simplified icon lookup instead of importing `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:30-44`; `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:56-61` regex-matches `GitService.ts` source rather than exercising NUL records. Consequently, multi-part/partial/default lookup and path parsing regressions can pass. The untracked command at `src/main/git/GitService.ts:121-124` also lacks `-z`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:160-162` splits and trims lines, corrupting valid untracked filenames containing newlines or leading/trailing spaces. **Smallest fix:** test an imported/compiled production lookup, extract an executable pure name-status parser, switch untracked listing to `git ls-files -z`, and cover Unicode, whitespace/newlines, rename scores, copies, and nested worktrees.

### Correct

- Final `package.json` and `package-lock.json` match `HEAD`; there is no new production dependency or lockfile churn. The vendored Seti data is attributed in `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/NOTICE.md:1-8` and includes the upstream MIT license in `LICENSE.md:1-21`.
- `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:17-28` now explicitly narrows the immutable JSON boundary and `npm run typecheck` passes.
- `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:30-50` correctly handles extensionless names and normalizes special-name label matching.
- `src/renderer/src/main.tsx:5-6` imports the scoped stylesheet after base styles. Trusted bundled SVG is rendered with a fallback at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4267-4280`, and the Seti color mapping is exhaustively typed at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:7-27`.
- Focused tests pass 6/6; `git diff --check` passes; no staged files are present.

### Validation Notes

- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `node --test tests/fileIcons.test.mjs`: passed, 6/6.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json`: no Seti/SVGO findings; four pre-existing high findings remain outside this diff.
- `npm run build`: TypeScript, main, and preload stages passed; the npm/Node process then exited 139 during renderer transformation. A full production build is therefore not validated in this workspace.
- An earlier aggregate `node --test tests/*.test.mjs` run had the Seti suite passing and three failures in unrelated existing tests; it was not rerun after the final corrections.
- No manual Electron UI check was performed; narrow widths, dark mode, keyboard focus, and screen-reader behavior remain residual risks.

details._piDeckOriginalContent:
## Review

Blocking findings are present. The current diff is not ready for an upstream PR.

### Blocker

- **The new vendored Seti module does not typecheck, so the required build cannot pass.** `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:25` asserts imported JSON directly to `SetiDefinitions`, but TypeScript infers each JSON mapping value as `string[]`, which is not safely comparable to the fixed two-element `IconDetails` tuple. `npm run typecheck` fails with TS2352 at this line. The focused test still passes because `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:5-16` parses the JSON independently and never imports or compiles `index.ts`. **Smallest fix:** either validate/narrow the JSON structure, provide JSON declarations that preserve tuple types, or, if the bundled immutable data is accepted as trusted, make the explicit boundary `rawDefinitions as unknown as SetiDefinitions`; then rerun typecheck and the full build.

### High

- **Git badge paths are wrong when a project directory is a subdirectory of a Git worktree.** `src/main/git/GitService.ts:113-124` executes Git from the supplied project `cwd`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:135` resolves every returned path against that `cwd`. Verified in this repository: `git -C src diff --name-status -z` still returned repository-root-relative entries such as `package.json` and `src/main/git/GitService.ts`; the current code would create `F:\PiDeck\src\package.json` and `F:\PiDeck\src\src\main\git\GitService.ts`. Those paths cannot match file-tree nodes, so the map at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4041-4047` silently produces no badges for nested projects and changed-file actions point to wrong paths. **Smallest fix:** resolve `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` once, run the listing commands from that root, join returned paths to that root, and filter to the selected project subtree if the drawer is intended to remain project-scoped. Add a nested-`cwd` integration test.

### Medium

- **Directory tooltips are now mislabeled as files.** `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4296` computes `getFileTypeLabel(node.name)` for both files and directories; `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4321-4324` appends that value to directory titles. Because extensionless names return `FILE` at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:39-40`, a normal `src` directory is exposed as `src\nFILE`; dotted directories can receive arbitrary extension labels. **Smallest fix:** compute and append the type label only in the file branch. Keep the directory title as its relative path (or add a localized directory description if product requirements call for one).

- **The new status/type UI is incomplete for localization, keyboard use, and non-color perception.** User-visible category text is hardcoded at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:33-50`, including `FILE`, rather than going through `i18n.ts`. The badge at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4310-4313` is an empty span whose added/modified/renamed state is conveyed only by colors at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:87-90`; neither the button title nor an accessible description exposes the status. The label CSS at `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:103-105` reacts to hover but not keyboard focus. **Smallest fix:** keep true technical identifiers (TypeScript, JSON, TSX) as data, localize generic categories and Git status names, add status text through an accessible description or visually hidden span, mark decorative SVG/badge content `aria-hidden`, mirror hover with `.file-node-row:focus-visible .file-node-type-label`, and respect reduced motion for the opacity transition.

- **The Git parser tests inspect source text rather than behavior and omit edge cases the code claims to handle.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:56-61` only regex-matches implementation source, so it passes despite the nested-root defect. The untracked command at `src/main/git/GitService.ts:121-124` also lacks `-z`, while `src/main/git/GitService.ts:160-162` splits on newlines and trims; valid untracked names containing newlines or leading/trailing spaces are corrupted. **Smallest fix:** extract/export a pure NUL-status parser, use `git ls-files -z` for untracked files, and execute tests for spaces, Unicode, tabs/newlines, rename scores, copies, staged/unstaged duplicates, and nested working directories.

- **The focused Seti lookup test duplicates a simplified implementation instead of testing the production lookup.** `tests/fileIcons.test.mjs:12-16` checks only an exact filename or one final suffix and does not execute `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:30-44`, including multi-part extensions and partial matches. This is why the focused suite is green while production TypeScript is broken. **Smallest fix:** make the lookup test import compiled production code through the repository's established test loader/build path, or factor the runtime lookup into a directly executable module. Cover multi-part extensions, partials, default fallback, and malformed/missing icon data.

### Low

- **Seti lookup remains case-sensitive, so uppercase extensions fall back to the generic icon.** `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:21-23` forwards the original name, and `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:30-42` performs exact case-sensitive lookups. Runtime checks against the source data showed uppercase `App.TSX`/`README.MD` miss mappings that lowercase equivalents resolve. **Smallest fix:** normalize the lookup name to lowercase (the data recognizes lowercase `dockerfile` and `makefile`) and add uppercase-extension tests.

- **The new type-label column permanently reserves invisible width.** `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:49-50` adds an `auto` third grid column, while `src/renderer/src/file-icons.css:93-104` hides it only with `opacity`. This correctly avoids hover layout shift, but on a narrow right drawer a long label such as `TypeScript` still truncates the filename even when the label is invisible. **Smallest fix:** cap the label width and hide it below a drawer/container-width threshold, preserving the stable grid and no-text-jump rule.

### Correct

- The dependency/lockfile issue discovered in the initial snapshot has been removed: final `package.json` and `package-lock.json` match `HEAD`, and the implementation now uses a local Seti snapshot. `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/NOTICE.md:1-8` identifies both upstream sources and `LICENSE.md:1-21` includes the MIT license. The final audit contains no `seti-icons`/SVGO findings; its four high findings are pre-existing dependencies outside this diff.
- `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:30-50` now handles extensionless names without the prior `slice(-1)` empty-label bug and normalizes special-name label matching.
- `src/renderer/src/main.tsx:5-6` imports the dedicated stylesheet after base styles, so its scoped overrides apply predictably.
- `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4267-4280` renders only trusted bundled SVG and retains a `FileText` fallback. The color mapping is exhaustively typed at `src/renderer/src/fileIcons.ts:7-27`.
- Recursive Git status maps are forwarded at `src/renderer/src/components/app/AppParts.tsx:4333-4341`, and existing row hover/active/focus-visible styling remains at `src/renderer/src/styles.css:6362-6371`.
- `git diff --check` passes. No staged files were present at the end of review. Project/source files were not modified by this reviewer; only this required artifact was written.

### Validation Notes

- `node --test tests/fileIcons.test.mjs`: passed, 6/6, but it does not compile/import the production TypeScript lookup.
- `npm run typecheck`: **failed** with TS2352 at `src/renderer/src/vendor/seti-icons/index.ts:25`.
- A full current-snapshot build was not rerun because its first `tsc --noEmit` step is already known to fail. Earlier build attempts, before the concurrent vendoring correction, also did not complete due workspace/Monaco issues and are not evidence for the final snapshot.
- `npm audit --omit=dev --registry=https://registry.npmjs.org --json`: reports four pre-existing high-severity dependency findings and no Seti/SVGO chain.
- `node --test tests/*.test.mjs` was run before the final concurrent vendoring correction: the Seti tests passed, while three unrelated existing tests failed. It is not a final-snapshot green run.
- No manual Electron UI check was performed; narrow widths, dark mode, keyboard focus, and screen-reader output remain residual risks.
